name: hcportal_test
description: Test stored procedure that retrieves the top 5 event names for a given kennel, ordered by event start date descending. This appears to be a simple test/debugging SP used to verify kennel access and event retrieval.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: User's public hasher ID. Not actually used in the SP body (dead input).

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token. Not actually used in the SP body (dead input).

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: The public kennel ID to filter events by. Used to join Event to Kennel.

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Not used in the SP body (dead input).

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Not used in the SP body (dead input).

rowsets.name: EventNames
rowsets.description: Top 5 event names for the specified kennel, ordered by start date descending

columns.name: EventName
columns.description: The name of the event from HC.Event table

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. This is a read-only query so it's less critical, but should follow standards.
The SP has no error handling whatsoever. Any runtime error will propagate unhandled to the caller.
Despite accepting @publicHasherId and @accessToken as parameters, the SP performs no authentication checks. Any caller can access any kennel's data by passing the correct @publicKennelId.
@publicHasherId, @accessToken, @ipAddress, and @ipGeoDetails are all accepted but never used in the SP body. Only @publicKennelId is actually used.
The SP name and commented EXEC example (line 8) suggest this is a test/debugging procedure that may not be intended for production use.
Line 8: Contains a commented-out EXEC statement with a hardcoded GUID that looks like a real publicKennelId. Leftover from development/debugging.

codeSmells.issue: Silent empty result on invalid kennel
codeSmells.detail: If @publicKennelId is NULL or doesn't match any kennel, the SP returns an empty rowset with no error indication.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
